summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture of a relaxed second set featuring rare “If I Had the World to Give” and soaring versions of Eyes of the World, Estimated Prophet and Aiko.
review: The SBD recording is praised for its squeaky‑clean mix and excellent instrument separation, letting Jerry’s guitar tone and Keith’s keyboards shine. Musically the show is a treasure trove of first‑time performances – Stagger Lee, I Need a Miracle and the ultra‑rare “If I Had the World to Give” – with standout jams on Eyes of the World, Estimated Prophet and Aiko, though some reviewers note sloppy moments on Miracle.

ai_rating.rationale: Average user rating ≈4.2★ across 17 reviews; most comments are enthusiastic about both the performance and the clean soundboard, with only a few isolated criticisms.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Consistently described as squeaky‑clean, crisp, with clear instrument separation and good levels; no major dropouts or muffling reported.

standout_songs: If I Had the World to Give
show_quality.setlist_flow: Second‑set only, relaxed vibe; opens with new material (Stagger Lee, I Need a Miracle) then moves into deep jams on Estimated→Other One→Eyes and culminates in the rare World‑to‑Give/Aiko sequence.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar tone and vocal phrasing are repeatedly praised; his solo on World to Give is called “heart‑wrenching,” though one reviewer felt he sounded “asleep.”
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s bass work in Space receives kudos for its “bombs” and humming quality; a few notes about bass being buried do not appear here.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir’s slide licks are highlighted as a highlight of the set; his rhythm playing is generally solid.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s keyboard contributions are called “fine work,” with notable solos leading into Aiko and other jams.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drum jam at the end of World to Give and the extended drum/space sections receive positive remarks for energy and clarity.
